reasoning: Anish Giri's official Elo on April 12, 2026, is 2753. A 1% jump requires reaching 2781. FIDE list updates are monthly, so the April 19 rating will definitively stay 2753. Gaining 28+ Elo points implies performing exceptionally well across multiple elite tournaments (earning roughly +6 above expected score). Although Giri is doing well at the active 2026 Candidates with an estimated +10 points to his live rating, closing the remaining gap to 2781 by May-October 2026 is highly improbable given his tendency to hold rating across normal supertournaments. Moving into the 1 to 3 year forecasts, there's a higher chance he could hit a hot streak to reclaim a top-3 level (reminiscent of recent surges by peers like Nakamura or Caruana), pushing probabilities incrementally higher. For very distant forecasts (2031, 2036), Giri will be in his late 30s to early 40s. Age-related decline would typically keep him from 2781 without significant FIDE rating deflation adjustments or broader inflation, pulling probabilities heavily toward absolute uncertainty.
